author: Przemek Chojecki / Codex audit
title: Paper D v7 adds the first-grid MCA cap
summary: Paper D v7 keeps the v6 self-contained universal cap, extends the no-loss CA endpoint from f<n-k-1 to f<=n-k-1, and adds a quotient-remainder/first-grid theorem showing that large official-envelope rows are already CA/MCA unsafe at delta=1-rho-1/n.
impact: The public Paper D cap rows now cite v7. The leaderboard also has new PROVED_PAPERD_V7_FIRST_GRID rows for the four official rates, separate from the older fixed-divisor universal-cap rows.

author: Vadim Avdeev / Codex audit
title: Finite-slope floors now cover a=257..288 on the F_17^32 row
summary: PRs #134 and #135 add random simple-pole and coset-packet finite-slope lower floors for RS[F_17^32,H,256]. The strongest near-capacity rows give all finite slopes at a=257 and a=258; coset packets continue with explicit binomial lower floors through a=288.
impact: This strengthens the finite-slope MCA lower-bound ledger without changing the known delta* cap. The rows are lower-bound proof records only, not safe-side theorems or protocol claims.

author: Scott Hughes / Codex audit
title: Hybrid quotient-residual packet clears the interleaved-list gate at a=326
summary: PR #133 contributes a standard interleaved-list lower bound Lambda_mu(C,326) >= 7 for the F_17^32, n=512, k=256 row, plus high-agreement route cuts and supporting verifiers.
impact: This is a separate list-track row over denominator |F|=17^32. It does not add an MCA N_bad numerator or a protocol soundness claim.

author: AllenGrahamHart and Scott Hughes / Codex audit
title: Boundary-off normal form and a=507 bridge route cut integrated conservatively
summary: PR #131 is distilled to a local external-anchor Hankel normal form for the M1 proof program. PR #132 records that adjacent 6+1 line/list arithmetic cannot be counted as a seventh finite-slope support-wise event at a=507.
impact: Both are useful guardrails, but neither is a new public leaderboard row.

author: Przemek Chojecki / Codex audit
title: Paper D v6 adds the prize-facing completion program
summary: Paper D v6 kept the v5 universal MCA cap constants and CS25-free proof route, tightened the conversion collision-count derivation, and added the integer-staircase formulation plus conditional MCA/list completion theorems for turning the cap into a full threshold determination.
impact: Historical version-promotion note: v6 made the cap self-contained and prize-facing. v7 now supersedes it as the public Paper D source.

author: Codex
title: Papers B v4, C v5, and D v7 are now the public theory set
summary: Paper B v4 promotes the high-agreement line/list/curve boundary layer, Paper C v5 adds the theorem-backed protocol ledger compiler, and Paper D v7 makes the headline MCA universal cap self-contained while adding the first-grid cap.
impact: The leaderboard impact is both status and cap strength: verified Paper D cap rows are now PROVED_PAPERD_V7_CAP, first-grid rows are PROVED_PAPERD_V7_FIRST_GRID, and list-cap rows cite Paper B v4.

author: Arnon-Boneh-Fenzi
title: Proximity Prize MCA benchmark regimes
summary: Table 1 in Open Problems in List Decoding and Correlated Agreement separates the MCA landscape into zero-radius, unique-decoding, Johnson-radius, and capacity-edge regimes.
impact: The current RS-MCA frontier is in the capacity-edge lower-bound regime, not the Johnson-radius positive upper-bound regime.

author: Codex scanner audit
title: Prime192 scanner rows instantiate Paper D v7 across all four rates
summary: The leaderboard_sweep_192 packet contributes four concrete q≈2^192 smooth subgroup rows with k=2^40, one for each official rate, plus a small F_17^32 Paper D example. They instantiate the proved Paper D v7 cap with exact primes q = 1 mod n and +87 bit margins on the prime192 rows.
impact: These are scanner-backed examples of the Paper D v7 cap, not new theorems beyond Paper D and not explicit slope censuses. The scanner verifies the divisor, binomial, and field ledgers for each displayed cap row.

author: Codex
title: Finite-row MCA threshold note packages the 506/507 gate
summary: The F_17^32, n=512, k=256 row now has a standalone threshold note: finite-slope support-wise MCA is safe at agreement 507 and unsafe at agreement 506.
impact: This supersedes the old strict264-next plan for pinning the finite row. The remaining prize work is definition audit plus the row-independent high-agreement threshold compiler.

author: AllenGrahamHart / Codex audit
title: All-line Hankel aperiodic packet distilled but not promoted
summary: PR #127 is a large M1 packet around Hankel-pencil and aperiodic residue-line packing. The integration keeps only a concise audit/proof-program note instead of merging the whole generated packet.
impact: Potentially important for M1, but no all-line upper bound is promoted until the branch bounds and imported finite computations are independently checkable.

author: AllenGrahamHart / Codex audit
title: Variable-line packet lemmas reduce the M1 residual branch
summary: PR #130 adds local Hankel variable-line packet lemmas: non-fixed packet mass is charged to quotient defects, active domain singletons, and different-slope two-exchange codegree; in the high-agreement rate-half range the zero-lower singleton class vanishes.
impact: Useful M1 infrastructure, but not a final all-line residue-packing theorem and not a leaderboard row. The remaining targets are polynomial bounds for active codegree and one-outside boundary target images.

author: AllenGrahamHart
title: Challenge-map pullback ledgers for adjacent high-agreement objects
summary: PR #122 adds high-agreement adjacent ledgers for challenge-map pullbacks, keeping line, CA/projective, curve, list, folding/query, and denominator ledgers separate.
impact: Useful for future protocol-facing calculators; not a new MCA numerator by itself.

author: AllenGrahamHart
title: Arbitrary-anchor residue-cloud floors
summary: PR #126 sharpens the F1 arbitrary-anchor split: residue-cloud floors must be separated into common-core, dense-overlap, and free-packing branches.
impact: This is a warning against naive base-to-extension MCA lifts. It does not add a leaderboard numerator but gives a cleaner checklist for extension-valued line theorems.

author: Vadim / Codex audit
title: L1 monomial dyadic descent survivor families
summary: PR #129 records monomial-prefix dyadic descent survivor families and a local n=16 audit checklist/verifier.
impact: Good bounded toy-model evidence for quotient-periodic survivor structure, but not an arbitrary-word locator local limit and not a protocol result.

author: Scott Hughes
title: L1 d=3 proper-subgroup twisted collision bound
summary: PR #125 adds the d=3 proper-subgroup prefix-dual twisted collision estimate, with the standard Katz/Gauss input kept explicit and an input-audit note beside it.
impact: Useful L1 infrastructure for generated-field locator local limits. It is not a leaderboard MCA row and does not prove the arbitrary-word L1 theorem.

author: AllenGrahamHart / Claude / Codex audit
title: Reserve272/288/313 targets are now proved lower-bound rows
summary: The moving-root tangent floor gives LD_sw(C,a) >= n-a+1 at a=272,288,313, namely 241,225,200 bad slopes. Strict352 independently covers the same range by quotient-core floors.
impact: These rows should be displayed as subsumed proof records, not open search targets and not independent reserve-scale local-limit theorems.

author: AllenGrahamHart / Claude / Codex audit
title: Cycle116/119 gates no longer need Cycle84 density
summary: The moving-root tangent floor gives LD_sw(C,262) >= 251 and LD_sw(C,263) >= 250 for C = RS[F_17^32,H,256]. Since floor(17^32/2^128)=6, the >2^-128 gates at delta=125/256 and 249/512 do not depend on the Cycle84 census.
impact: Keep 52,747,567,092 as the exact-density computation-dependent record; use the tangent floor for the unconditional threshold gate.

author: Codex audit
title: Proximity certificate scanner prototype
summary: The scanner turns a row/config JSON into JSON and Markdown reports for entropy reserve, quotient profile, Paper D cap hypotheses, high-agreement line/list/curve ledgers, and combined protocol-ledger verdicts.
impact: Use it as the first replayable audit before adding public board rows. It makes q_gen, q_line, q_chal, quotient divisors, endpoint agreements, and theorem-backed versus conditional terms explicit, but it does not prove extension-line MCA or the missing local-limit theorems.

author: Codex audit
title: Tangent257 capacity-edge MCA row
summary: The tangent lower-floor theorem gives LD_sw(C,257) >= 256 for C = RS[F_17^32,H,256], so epsilon_mca(C,255/512) >= 256/17^32.
impact: This is now the closest-to-capacity theorem-backed finite MCA row on the public board, clearing 2^-128 by +5.20 bits. It is still only a finite-slope support-wise MCA lower floor, not an exact threshold or protocol soundness statement.

author: Codex
title: Strict264 quotient-floor proof record
summary: The strict264 quotient-core construction uses the map x -> x^8, an augmented-code list of size binom(64,33), and deep-point conversion to produce at least nine support-wise bad slopes at agreement 264.
impact: This remains a quotient-core mechanism record. The moving-root tangent floor now gives stronger lower bounds at agreement 264, so 265 is not the threshold-pinning target.

author: Codex
title: Strict352 dyadic quotient-core proof record
summary: The strict352 dyadic quotient-core construction proves LD_sw(C,a) >= 7 for every agreement 264 <= a <= 352, and LD_sw(C,352) >= 16 for C = RS[F_17^32,H,256].
impact: This was the quotient-core frontier before the tangent staircase was isolated. It remains a useful mechanism record, but agreement 353 is solved by the generic tangent floor.

author: GPT-5.5 Pro / Codex audit
title: Tangent-star barrier closes the finite-slope non-tangent branch
summary: The moving-root tangent floor is exact in the high-agreement range, and the tangent-star refinement classifies extremizers there.
impact: For RS[F_17^32,H,256], |H|=512, this gives LD_sw(C,506)=7 and LD_sw(C,507)=6 and rules out any seventh finite-slope support-wise MCA branch at agreement a>=507. Adjacent CA/projective/curve/list ledgers are now recorded separately.

author: GPT-5.5 Pro / Codex audit
title: Adjacent high-agreement ledgers pin CA/projective/curve/list objects
summary: The high-agreement tangent staircase extends to no-loss CA, projective slopes, finite-parameter degree-d curve CA/MCA, and MDS interleaved-list uniqueness.
impact: For RS[F_17^32,H,256], |H|=512, the line-plus-list coding numerator is 514-a. With no query/folding loss, this is unsafe at agreement a=507 and safe at a=508; protocol use still needs explicit challenge-field, folding, query, extension-lift, and cryptographic ledgers.

author: GPT-5.5 Pro / Codex audit
title: Generalized high-agreement ledgers go beyond the F_17^32 row
summary: The adjacent-ledger formulas are rewritten for arbitrary RS[F,D,k] rows using R=n-k, r=n-a, and the denominator budget B_Q=floor(Q/2^128).
impact: In the exact tangent range, line/CA/projective numerators are r+1, degree-d curve numerators are d(r+1), and interleaved-list uniqueness contributes one. For prize-scale k=2^40, the tangent method pins line thresholds only up to roughly 166-170 field bits, depending on rate; larger fields need quotient, entropy, curve/folding, or local-limit ledgers.

author: Scott Hughes
title: L1 d=2 cubic subgroup twisted bound
summary: PR #121 proves a proper-subgroup H^{2k} cubic collision estimate using Fourier reconstruction, multiplicative-character expansion of 1_H, and one-variable Gauss/Kummer-Artin-Schreier input.
impact: This is useful L1 infrastructure for generated-field local limits, but it is not a new MCA leaderboard row or a general higher-moment theorem.

author: Scott Hughes
title: L1 odd-moment Hooley-Katz audit
summary: PR #120 proves the full-affine projective odd-moment collision geometry and audits the Hooley-Katz/Ghorpade-Lachaud constant route.
impact: The audit keeps full-affine, full-torus, and proper-subgroup ledgers separate and shows why the generic full-affine point-count path is not yet enough for the subgroup L1 reserve-scale problem.

author: AllenGrahamHart
title: F1 syndrome-pencil normal form
summary: PR #118 gives a Hankel-pencil incidence test for support-wise line explanations and a matching noncontainment criterion.
impact: This replaces vague extension-line language with an exact locator equation over the actual line field. It is theory infrastructure, not a completed F1 theorem.

author: AllenGrahamHart
title: L2 interleaved codegree reduction
summary: PR #108 proves a two-regime and recursive reduction from interleaved lists to base-code lists at agreements a and 2a-k.
impact: The reduction is unconditional; the desired L2 exponent saving still needs an L1-family tail-list bound.

author: AllenGrahamHart
title: Paper D MCA cap gets a CS25-free route
summary: PR #115 isolates a simple-pole/deep-point averaging proof route for the headline MCA cap under Paper D's finite-field hypotheses.
impact: The MCA cap no longer has to load-bear on the CS25 import, though CA/list statements still need the external import audit.

author: AllenGrahamHart
title: Seven-slope arithmetic gate for strict264
summary: PR #110 confirmed the arithmetic gate: over F_17^32, seven retained slopes at agreement 264 clear 2^-128. The newer quotient-floor proof supplies at least nine slopes.
impact: Kept as the arithmetic gate, but no longer the frontier status. The strict264 row is now a proof record.

author: Scott Hughes
title: Large-domain mixed-Weil energy floor
summary: PR #119 adds a possible L1 route cut for large-domain prefix-dual energy, kept conditional pending the mixed Weil citation and constant check.
impact: Useful as an audit target for L1; not promoted as a theorem and not included as a frontier estimate.

title: Cycle119/Cycle120 ABF-row obstruction
summary: The active exact-density numerator remains 52,747,567,092 bad challenges over F_17^32 and is still computation-dependent. Separately, the moving-root tangent floor proves the >2^-128 gates at agreements 262 and 263 without using that numerator.
impact: Use the tangent floor for unconditional gate language; use Cycle84/116/119 only for the stronger exact-density record after independent finite-count review.

title: Standalone Cycle120 LDsw proof note
summary: PR #105 adds a compact standalone note for the source-scoped lower bound LD_sw(RS[F_17^32,H,256],262) >= 52,747,567,092.
impact: This is the human-review target that replaces a large raw generated packet for public communication.

title: Exact MCA to line-decoding normalization
summary: PR #102 records the support-wise identity epsilon_mca(C,delta) = LD_sw(C,ceil((1-delta)n))/|F| for the two-source object.
impact: Future updates should use this bridge to avoid denominator and endpoint drift.

title: Extension-line lower floors
summary: PR #103 strengthens the F1 warning: fixed-rate extension-line families have floors on the order of (1-rho)^2/2 for sigma one and (1-rho)^(sigma+1)/(sigma+1)! at fixed sigma before extension-degree dilution.
impact: Naive base-to-extension transfer remains false without an explicit theorem.

title: M1 beta-pushforward spectral audit
summary: PR #104 scans beta-pushforward rows and finds no hidden growing p^2 mass in the tested windows.
impact: This supports the current M1 conductor-style proof target but does not prove the missing bounded-conductor estimate.

title: L1 quotient-budgeted locator route
summary: PRs #99 and #106 add characteristic-zero prefix reductions, bad-prime proof-record machinery, quotient-budgeted locator conjectures, and a full-list quotient proof program.
impact: This is the main positive-route infrastructure for generated-field locator local limits.

title: Sharp interleaved-list target
summary: PR #107 sharpens L2: interleaved-list analysis should charge quotient-core mass diagonally and avoid the naive Cartesian-product numerator.
impact: This turns a vague interleaving hazard into a finite set of support/codegree targets.

title: X1 bridges and Lean formalization stub
summary: PR #101 adds deep-point/interleaved bridge notes, quotient-reduction notes, prize-path maps, and a small Lean scaffold.
impact: This gives future agents a cleaner path from experimental notes toward mechanized statements.
